title: Move System/Command/State panel content to right panel

close_reason: Moved system-panel, command-panel, state-panel divs to right panel

title: Helper scripts for claude invocation
description: Shell scripts for common usage patterns: claude.sh wraps ccwrapper with default flags, agent-prompt.sh pipes a prompt file into claude via ccwrapper, clean-claude.sh resets Claude state by clearing config/cache directories.
close_reason: All features implemented and shipped

title: Style right panel tabs to match left panel
description: Ensure the right panel tab bar has consistent styling with the left panel tabs. The tabs should use the same CSS classes or equivalent styles. The Output tab should appear as the default active tab with the active style applied.
close_reason: Right panel tabs use same .tabs/.tab CSS classes as left panel, styled consistently via shared CSS rules

title: Update README.md build, run, and CLI flags sections
description: Update the Building section (currently accurate — Go, make build, web/ embedded). Update Running section to remove the old 'Open http://localhost:8080' comment — the server listens on 0.0.0.0:8080. Keep the --demo flag documentation. Remove the Container section (Containerfile no longer exists). Update the CLI flags section — the current flags are: --output-format stream-json, --verbose, --print, --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions, --dangerously-skip-permissions, --disable-slash-commands, --no-session-persistence, --mcp-config '' --strict-mcp-config, --disallowed-tools (AskUserQuestion, CronCreate, CronDelete, CronList, EnterPlanMode, ExitPlanMode, TodoWrite, Skill, NotebookEdit, EnterWorktree). Add a section about prompt files (prompts/plan.md, refine.md, execute.md) and how they are served via /api/prompts/{name}.
close_reason: Updated build, run, CLI flags; removed Container section; added prompt files section

title: Update README.md to reflect Plan/Refine/Execute workflow
description: The README.md is outdated. It describes ccwrapper as an ad-hoc chat tool with context accumulation. The project has been redesigned around a Plan → Refine → Execute workflow. The README needs a complete rewrite covering: (1) the new workflow model — Plan breaks work into epics via br, Refine asks clarifying questions, Execute picks up and implements epics; (2) the current tab layout (Plan, Output, Refine, Execute, System, Command, State); (3) prompt files in prompts/ (plan.md, refine.md, execute.md); (4) br/beads integration for issue tracking; (5) updated CLI flags; (6) removal of context accumulation and ad-hoc chat. This is a single-epic task: rewrite README.md to accurately describe the current project.
close_reason: All three sub-tasks completed: README.md fully rewritten for Plan/Refine/Execute workflow

title: Answer Question → Refine inline editing feature
description: When the user clicks 'Answer Question' on a collapsed turn in the Output tab, it should: (1) extract the latest assistant text reply from that turn, (2) switch to the Refine tab, (3) populate a new editable textarea in the Refine panel with that text so the user can edit/answer questions inline, and (4) show a 'Send Answers' button that sends the edited content as the next prompt to Claude. This epic covers all UI and backend changes needed.
close_reason: All sub-tasks completed: Answer Question button and Refine inline editor fully implemented

title: Add Answer tab and panel to HTML
description: Add a new 'Answer' tab to the .tabs container in index.html (after the Refine tab). The tab should initially be hidden/disabled and only become visible when the refine prompt completes.
close_reason: Added Answer tab, answer-panel with toolbar, textarea, answer.md preview, and CSS

title: Remove Refresh buttons from HTML and JS
description:
Remove the Refresh buttons from the Refine and Execute tabs in the web UI.

## Files to edit
- cmd/ccwrapper/web/index.html
- cmd/ccwrapper/web/app.js

## Changes required

### index.html
1. In the refine-panel (line ~310): Remove the `<button id="btn-refresh-br-refine" ...>Refresh</button>` element
2. In the execute-panel (line ~326): Remove the `<button id="btn-refresh-br" ...>Refresh</button>` element

### app.js
1. Remove the `const btnRefreshBr = document.getElementById('btn-refresh-br');` declaration (line ~16)
2. Remove the `const btnRefreshBrRefine = document.getElementById('btn-refresh-br-refine');` declaration (line ~20)
3. Remove `btnRefreshBr.addEventListener('click', loadBrList);` (line ~103)
4. Remove `btnRefreshBrRefine.addEventListener('click', loadBrListRefine);` (line ~132)

## Notes
- Keep the automatic loading on tab switch (loadBrList/loadBrListRefine called in tab click handler) — only remove the manual Refresh buttons
- Keep the loadBrList() and loadBrListRefine() functions themselves — they are still used
- After changes, run `make build` to verify the build succeeds
close_reason: Removed Refresh buttons from both Refine and Execute tabs in index.html and app.js

title: Frontend JS: Toggle Plan disabled state based on open issues
description: Add logic in app.js to: (1) After loading br-list, check the open_count from the API response. (2) If open_count > 0, set plan-editor textarea to disabled and btn-send-plan to disabled. (3) If open_count === 0, enable both. (4) Check on page load. (5) Re-check after sendPlan completes (since it creates issues). (6) Re-check after Scrap Work completes (since it removes issues). (7) Re-check after loadBrList/refresh. This ensures the Plan tab state is always in sync with whether open issues exist.
close_reason: Added updatePlanDisabledState function, called on page load, after loadBrList, and when SSE status shows !isRunning

title: Backend: Add /api/br-scrap endpoint
description:
Add a new POST endpoint /api/br-scrap to harness.go that:
1. Runs 'br list --json --status=open' to get all open work item IDs
2. Runs 'br delete --hard --force <ids>' to hard-delete all open items (removes tombstones from JSONL)
3. Returns JSON response with count of deleted items or error

Pattern: Follow the existing HandleBrList handler pattern in harness.go (lines ~240-256). Register the route in main.go alongside the existing GET /api/br-list route.

Files to edit:
- cmd/ccwrapper/harness.go (add HandleBrScrap method)
- cmd/ccwrapper/main.go (register POST /api/br-scrap route)
close_reason: Completed

title: Prepend answer.md contents to editor text in sendAnswers()

The final prompt sent should be: answerMdContents + '\n\n' + text (where text is the editor value). This ensures Claude receives the answer.md instructions along with the user's answers.

description: harness.go lines 261,349: json.Unmarshal errors silently ignored. Return proper HTTP error responses to frontend.

title: Epic: Split harness.go Into Focused Files
description: harness.go is 572 lines handling 8+ concerns. Split into: (1) harness.go - SSE client management and broadcast (core Harness struct), (2) handlers.go - HTTP handler methods (HandlePrompt, HandleBrList, HandleClaudeJSON, etc.), (3) process.go - Claude CLI process lifecycle (startClaude, processStream, killProcess), (4) cleanup.go - File system state cleanup (cleanClaudeState, cleanSessionFiles). Keep all files in the same package.

The button needs to be restored and adapted to work with the new tab-based UI (e.g., populate the Plan editor and switch to the Plan tab).

(2) Four nearly identical sendXxx() functions (sendRefine, sendAnswers, sendExecute, sendPlan) into a generic sendPrompt(type, content, callback). This reduces ~100 lines of duplication.

title: Remove Answer Questions button and auto-populate refine editor on refine response
description:
Currently, when a turn completes, an 'Answer Questions' button is added to the turn summary in the Output tab. The user must click it to populate the Refine editor. Instead:

1. Remove the addAnswerButton() call from collapseTurn() and remove the addAnswerButton/answerQuestion functions entirely
2. Remove the .btn-answer CSS styles from index.html
3. Track the prompt type that started the current turn (currentPrompt already exists: 'refine', 'answers', 'plan', 'execute')
4. When a turn completes (status event with running:false) AND currentPrompt is 'refine', automatically gather all text blocks from the turn and populate the refine editor
5. Switch to the Refine tab automatically
6. Show the refine editor and Send Answers button

Key files:
- cmd/ccwrapper/web/app.js: collapseTurn(), addAnswerButton(), answerQuestion(), SSE status handler
- cmd/ccwrapper/web/index.html: .btn-answer CSS
close_reason: Removed addAnswerButton/answerQuestion functions, removed .btn-answer CSS, added autoPopulateRefineEditor that runs when currentPrompt is refine on turn completion

title: Refine workflow: auto-populate editor on refine response

This makes the Refine flow interactive. When 'Send Plan' is pressed, the response should NOT populate the Refine tab — Plan is non-interactive.

Issues:
1. Track which prompt type triggered the current response (refine vs plan vs execute vs answers)
2. When a refine response completes, automatically gather text blocks and populate the refine editor
3. Remove the 'Answer Questions' button from turn displays on the Output tab
4. Make the refine editor fill available screen space when populated (currently too small due to min-height:12rem and display:none toggling)

close_reason: Updated app.js: removed brListContentRefine, loadBrListRefine, initial loads. Added workitems-panel tab click trigger and Refresh button handler.

title: Epic: Create Answer Tab
description: Create a new Answer tab that separates the answer-editing functionality from the Refine tab. Currently the Refine tab plays a dual role: it sends the refine prompt, then transforms into an answer editor. Instead, the Refine tab should stay as-is (prompt preview + Send Refine button only), and a new Answer tab should host the text editor, answer.md preview, and Send Answers button. The Answer tab should only appear/activate after a refine prompt completes and Claude returns with questions.
close_reason: Epic complete: Answer tab created, JS logic moved, Refine tab cleaned up

title: Makefile build system
description: Simple Makefile with build (darwin/arm64 via CGO), install (copies to /usr/local/bin), and clean targets. Produces a single static binary.
close_reason: All features implemented and shipped

title: Consolidate error response patterns in HTTP handlers
description: harness.go uses 3 different error patterns: log+silent, http.Error, JSON encode. Pick one pattern for user-facing handler errors (JSON with status code) and one for internal errors (log). Apply consistently across HandlePrompt, HandleBrList, HandleBrUpdate, HandleClaudeJSON, HandleSavePrompt.

title: Make refine editor fill available screen space
description:
The refine editor textarea (#refine-editor) currently has min-height:12rem and is styled inline. When populated with Claude's response, it should fill all available vertical space in the refine panel, similar to how #plan-editor uses flex:1 to fill its panel.

Changes needed:
1. Change #refine-panel from using generic .panel styles (display:block when active) to use flexbox layout like #plan-panel (display:flex, flex-direction:column, flex:1)
2. Update the refine-editor to use flex:1 instead of min-height:12rem so it grows to fill available space
3. Move the refine-preview section below the editor but ensure the editor gets priority space
4. When the editor is shown, it should dominate the panel height; when hidden, the preview can take the space

Key files:
- cmd/ccwrapper/web/index.html: #refine-panel structure and CSS
- cmd/ccwrapper/web/app.js: answerQuestion() shows the editor
close_reason: Changed #refine-panel to flexbox layout like #plan-panel, refine-editor uses flex:1 instead of min-height:12rem, preview section capped at 30% max-height

title: Epic: Update JS for always-visible output panel
description: Update app.js so the output panel is always visible in the right panel. Remove 'switch to Output tab' logic from all send functions (sendPlan, sendExecute, sendRefine, sendAnswers). Remove the Output tab click handler. Ensure auto-scroll and pending indicators still target the output panel correctly.
close_reason: All subtasks complete - JS already updated for always-visible output panel in two-panel layout

title: Epic: Add Scrap Work button to Work Items tab
description: Add a 'Scrap Work' button to the Work Items tab that deletes all open work items from br. This is an abort mechanism for when a user wants to bail on a plan. The button should hard-delete items (not close them) so they don't appear in history. Requires: 1) Backend API endpoint to list open items and delete them, 2) Frontend button with confirmation dialog, 3) UI refresh after deletion.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add scanner.Err() check after stream processing loop
description: harness.go ~line 472: after the scanner.Scan() loop in processStream, check scanner.Err() and log any I/O errors that occurred during scanning.

title: Running token and cost totals across sessions
description: Tracks cumulative input/output/cache tokens and estimated cost across the entire ccwrapper session, persisting totals even as Claude's internal counters reset between conversations. Displayed in the tab bar of the web UI.
close_reason: All features implemented and shipped

title: State tab for inspecting Claude config paths
description: Web UI tab that reads and displays contents of ~/.claude/, ~/.claude.json, and ~/.cache/ directories. Lets users inspect Claude Code's configuration and cached state without leaving the dashboard.
close_reason: All features implemented and shipped

title: PTY harness for Claude Code CLI
description: Go-based PTY wrapper that spawns claude CLI as a child process, captures raw terminal output via a pseudoterminal, and forwards it to both the real terminal and a web UI via SSE. Handles signal forwarding (SIGINT, SIGWINCH) and clean shutdown.
close_reason: All features implemented and shipped

title: Move Work Items HTML into Execute panel
description: In index.html, merge the workitems-panel content into execute-panel. Layout: Execute prompt/button at top, then work items below with some spacing (no divider needed). The work items section should show Refresh and Scrap Work buttons in their own row, then the work items list below. Keep the Execute button visually separated from the Scrap Work button to prevent accidental clicks. The work items list should take whatever space it needs (no max-height/scroll) since the buttons are above and already visible. Remove the workitems-panel div and the Work Items tab button from the tab bar. The tab label stays as 'Execute'. Key files: cmd/ccwrapper/web/index.html
close_reason: Merged work items HTML into execute panel, removed Work Items tab

title: Add inline text editor and Send Answers button to Refine panel
description: Redesign the Refine panel to support inline answer editing. Add a <textarea> element (id='refine-editor') to the Refine panel, styled like the plan-editor (monospace, flex:1, full height). This textarea is hidden by default and shown when the user clicks 'Answer Question'. Add a 'Send Answers' button (id='btn-send-answers') to the refine toolbar, also hidden by default. When 'Answer Question' is clicked: (1) the textarea is populated with the assistant's last reply text, (2) the textarea and Send Answers button become visible, (3) the existing refine-preview and br-list sections can remain visible below. When 'Send Answers' is clicked: (1) take the textarea content as the prompt, (2) send it via POST /api/prompt, (3) switch to Output tab and start a new turn. The existing 'Send Refine' button should remain and work independently. Files to edit: cmd/ccwrapper/web/index.html (new textarea + button HTML), cmd/ccwrapper/web/app.js (answerQuestion function, send answers handler, DOM references).
close_reason: Added refine-editor textarea, btn-send-answers button, sendAnswers handler, and answerQuestion integration

title: Move Plan tab toolbar HTML from bottom to top of panel

Currently the structure is: textarea then toolbar. It should be: toolbar then textarea, matching Refine and Execute tabs. The toolbar contains the explanation text ('Your text will be appended to plan.md and sent to Claude') and the Send button (#btn-send-plan).

title: Epic: Define Event Type Constants
description: Replace hardcoded event type strings throughout parser.go, harness.go, and app.js with named constants. Go side: define const block for message roles ('system', 'assistant', 'user') and event types ('result', 'rate_limit_event'). JS side: define const object for UI event types ('text', 'thinking', 'tool_use', 'tool_result', 'status', 'command', 'error'). Prevents typo bugs and enables exhaustiveness checking.

title: Handle json.Marshal errors in broadcast and sendInitialState
description: harness.go lines 97,99,114: json.Marshal errors are silently dropped with _. Log the error and return/skip instead of sending empty data.

The br list data should still load automatically when the tabs are opened — only the manual Refresh buttons are removed.

title: Disable Plan tab when open br issues exist
description: Make the Plan text editor and Send Plan button disabled/greyed out whenever there are open br issues. Plan is never additive — if users want to amend the plan, they must Scrap Work and start over. The user must Refine+Execute or Scrap Work before Plan becomes functional again. This enforces the intended workflow: Plan → Work Items created → Refine/Execute or Scrap → Plan again.
close_reason: All subtasks complete: backend open_count API, CSS disabled styles, JS toggle logic

title: Move Answer tab instructions above the editor
description: The instructional text (the span in answer-toolbar explaining prompts/answer.md behavior) is currently positioned below the text editor in the answer-panel. Move it above the answerEditor textarea so users see it before they start editing. This is a structural change in index.html — move the toolbar/hint element before the editor element in the DOM.
close_reason: Moved instructional text above the editor textarea, toolbar with buttons now below editor

title: Move output-panel div to right column
description: Move the #output-panel div out of the left panel's tab-content area and into the right column container. Set it to always be visible (display:block, no .panel class toggling needed). Give it flex:1 and overflow-y:auto.
close_reason: Moved output-panel div to right column, always visible with flex:1 and overflow-y:auto

title: Rewrite README.md intro and How It Works section
description: Replace the current intro and 'How it works' section. The old description says ccwrapper appends prompts to a context file and simulates multi-turn conversation. This is no longer true. The new intro should explain: ccwrapper is a web UI that wraps the Claude Code CLI around a structured Plan → Refine → Execute workflow. Each invocation is a single-shot --print call with no state carried between runs. The workflow uses prompt templates (prompts/plan.md, refine.md, execute.md) and beads_rust (br) for issue tracking. Plan prompts Claude to break work into epics in br. Refine prompts Claude to ask clarifying questions. Execute prompts Claude to implement the next ready epic, update br, and commit.
close_reason: Rewrote intro and How It Works section for Plan/Refine/Execute workflow

title: Add two-panel container to HTML
description: Wrap the existing body content (below header) in a flex container with two equal-width columns. The left column gets the tabs bar and all panels except Output. The right column gets the Output panel. Use display:flex with flex:1 on each side.
close_reason: Added main-container flex wrapper with left-panel and right-panel divs

title: Epic: Fix Go Error Handling Consistency
description: Fix dropped errors from json.Marshal/Unmarshal calls, add scanner.Err() check after stream processing loop, and consolidate the 3 different error reporting patterns (log+silent, http.Error, JSON response) into a consistent approach across harness.go and parser.go.
